title: Deposit Processing
body_text:
Deposit Processing
1	Introduction to Deposit Processing
1.1	Overview of Deposit Processing
This Part describes the preparation, delivery, and settlement of paper deposit items and the processing of remote deposit items with Branch Capture, Corporate Capture, and Mobile Deposit.
Branch Capture deposit processing allows your financial institution to scan images of paper deposit items and submit them to Central 1 electronically. See Chapter 9, Depositing Cheques in Branch Capture, for more information. If your financial institution uses an alternate deposit processor, see the documentation provided by that entity.
Corporate Capture deposit processing allows your business customers to scan images of paper items and submit them to Central 1 electronically. If you use real-time messaging, a credit is posted to your business customersâ accounts immediately (as an optional setting). If you donât implement real-time messaging, you can use the Corporate Capture Deposit File to credit your customersâ accounts automatically or manually. This file can also help with reconciling Corporate Capture deposits. See Chapter 15, Depositing Cheques in Corporate Capture, for more information.
Mobile deposits are cheques, money orders, drafts, and Receiver General Warrants that are deposited by customers to their accounts at your financial institution, using an application on a mobile device. The customer takes a picture of the front and back of the payment item. The image (as a clearing replacement document) is entered into the clearing in lieu of the physical item.
Financial institutions can accept mobile deposits with or without the use of real-time messaging. To maximize the benefits of this product, Central 1 recommends the use of real-time messaging because it allows for immediate settlement to customersâ accounts (as an optional setting). Financial institutions that donât implement real-time messaging can use the Mobile Deposit File to credit their customersâ accounts automatically. This deposit file also assists financial institutions with the reconciliation of Mobile Deposits. See Chapter 14, Mobile Deposits, for more information.
When customers deposit cheques, drafts, money orders, or Receiver General Warrants to their financial institution accounts or present the items for payment, the customers are credited and the payment items are sent to the drawee branch for further processing. Specifically, Central 1 sorts the deposit items by drawee branch and delivers the items to the appropriate data centres through the clearing. Items are delivered via the exchange of physical items (i.e., clearing replacement documents) or the exchange of image files. Your financial institutionâs account at Central 1 is credited. The drawee branch debits the drawersâ accounts.
If a deposit item is dishonoured by the drawee branch, the item is debited back to your financial institution. See the Incoming Clearing Items Part for information on chargebacks.
This Part provides an overview of cheque negotiation procedures, including special procedures for Receiver General Warrants and BC Employment and Assistance Cheques. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can refer to the Cheques Part for detailed information on negotiating cheques and obtaining endorsements.
Financial institutions use the Online Return System (ORS) to search for, view, and print deposit slips, cheques on deposit, deposit adjustments, and chargebacks. If your financial institution canât obtain the information that you need on ORS, you can initiate a trace request.

manuals@central1.com
or speak with your Central 1 Relationship Manager.
